arguments.query: Freshdesk developer portal API key OAuth
Freshdesk pricing plans free trial Sprout API key limitation
Freshdesk API key free trial sign up developer credentials self-serve
"Freshdesk uses Basic Access Authorization" API key authentication v2

researched_category.notes: Freshdesk is a cloud-based customer support ticketing platform (part of Freshworks).

one_liner.value: Cloud-based customer support and ticketing platform for managing help desk operations, tickets, conversations, and customer service workflows.

methods.notes: Freshdesk uses HTTP Basic Access Authorization. Primary method for agent integration is API key as username with any string (e.g. 'X') as password. Username/password Basic Auth also supported but deprecated/not recommended. Despite the docs referencing 'Basic', the actual credential used is an API key, making the effective mechanism 'API key via Basic Auth.' There is no OAuth for the Freshdesk inbound API; OAuth is only available for Freshdesk marketplace apps connecting to third-party services.

access.notes: API access is NOT available on the free Sprout plan. Requires a paid plan (Blossom/Growth $19/agent/month or higher). Signup is self-serve via Freshworks website with a 14-day free trial on the Enterprise plan. No admin approval is needed beyond having a paid account. No company email or phone verification required to sign up.

summary.value: Freshdesk has a well-documented public REST API (v2) covering tickets, contacts, agents, companies, groups, roles, solutions (knowledge base), discussions, conversations, time entries, email configs, canned responses, custom objects, satisfaction ratings, automations, SLA policies, business hours, products, and field service management. API endpoints are mapped to the account subdomain (e.g. https://domain.freshdesk.com/api/v2/). Rate limits apply per plan.
summary.notes: The v2 API is the current version. v1 API exists but is deprecated. The API is RESTful with JSON payloads.

agent_interface.notes: No official Freshdesk MCP server from Freshworks. However, there are several community MCP servers (effytech/freshdesk-mcp, NeuraLegion/freshdesk_mcp, CDataSoftware/freshdesk-mcp-server-by-cdata) and Composio offers a hosted Freshdesk MCP server with 178 tools. Composio's toolkit is the most comprehensive agent-facing integration available.

rationale.value: Freshdesk has a documented, broad public REST API (v2) with API key (Basic Auth) authentication. API credentials are self-serve via a paid subscription (no admin/gate approval beyond payment). Multiple community MCP servers already exist, and Composio provides a full MCP-hosted toolkit. The only material friction is that API access requires a paid plan (no free tier API access), but this is a standard SaaS gating model and does not block building an agent toolkit.
rationale.notes: The main consideration is that developers need a paid Freshdesk account to obtain API credentials, but they can get a 14-day free trial without a credit card. No admin approval, sales calls, or partnership agreements are required.

s1.quote: Freshdesk is a cloud-based customer support platform that was founded with the mission of enabling companies of all sizes to deliver better customer support.
s1.title: Freshdesk API Reference

s2.quote: Freshdesk Api Reference - Introduction - Overview What are APIs - What's New - Rate Limit - Authentication - Schema - Embedding - Attachments - Errors - Pagination - Best Practices - Code Samples
s2.title: Freshdesk API Reference

s3.quote: Freshdesk uses Basic Access Authorization. This means, for authentication, you can use the same username and password you use when logging into your helpdesk. Or, you can use your personal API key provided by us. If you use the API Key, there is no need for any password. You can use any set of characters as a dummy password.
s3.title: Help Desk API for Developers | Freshdesk (v1)

s4.quote: Freshdesk API doesn't support OAuth. It only support Basic authentication mechanism.
s4.title: How to use OAuth authentication mechanism for Freshdesk API? - Freshworks Developer Community

s5.quote: No, the access to Freshdesk APIs and the integration capabilities is not available in the free Sprout plan. It will be available from the Blossom plan onwards.
s5.title: Do we have API and integration capabilities in the free Sprout plan?

s7.quote: When you sign up, you instantly get access to our Enterprise plan for 14 days. We will alert you before your trial expires. You can then select a plan and pay by credit card to continue using the product.
s7.title: Freshdesk Pricing & Plans

s8.title: Help Desk API for Developers | Freshdesk (v1)

s9.quote: API Index listing resources: Tickets, Contacts, Agents, Companies, Groups, Roles, Solutions, Discussions, Conversations, Time Entries, Email Configs, Canned Responses, Custom Objects, Satisfaction Ratings, Automations, SLA Policies, Business Hours, Products, etc.
s9.title: Freshdesk API Reference - API Index

s10.quote: Freshdesk MCP for AI Agents — Securely connect your AI agents and chatbots (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc) with Freshdesk MCP or direct API to create tickets, update support requests, manage conversations, and automate customer support workflows through natural language.
s10.title: Freshdesk MCP Integration for AI Agents | Composio

s11.quote: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for Freshdesk API integration. 41 tools for managing support tickets, contacts, companies, and more.
s11.title: GitHub - NeuraLegion/freshdesk_mcp

s12.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation for Freshdesk API v2 integration. Provides tools for managing tickets, contacts, agents, companies, and conversations through the MCP interface.
s12.title: GitHub - Enreign/freshdeck-mcp

s13.quote: Read-only MCP Server allows you to connect to Freshdesk data from Claude Desktop through CData JDBC Drivers.
s13.title: GitHub - CDataSoftware/freshdesk-mcp-server-by-cdata

limitations: The Exa fetch for the v2 authentication section only returned the navigation sidebar text, not the full page body. However, the v1 docs and community sources confirm the same Basic Auth approach is used for v2.
No official Freshdesk MCP server exists from Freshworks themselves - only community and Composio-hosted implementations.
